columns.title: Design strict Nushell command routing for both agent shell tools
columns.goal: Design the owner-ratified strict-nu-on-both-agents routing using the research-proven two-swaps-plus-hard-gate model: (1) SYNTAX SWAP a vendored nushell-syntax skill so each agent emits Nushell not bash; (2) INTERPRETER ROUTING a profile-owned Nushell MCP execution tool (prefer built-in nu --mcp from Nushell 0.108+, else a pure-Rust nu-MCP crate honoring the no-C boundary) into which the model writes nu as a typed argument; (3) HARD GATE deny the built-in Bash tool so no bash escape remains. The nu -c PreToolUse rewrite hook is REJECTED as primary: broken by claude-code #4535 (dev-null injection) and #15897/#26506 (updatedInput), and it hands bash syntax to a non-POSIX nu parser (verified locally on nu 0.113.1: andand, dollar-paren, 2>&1, heredocs all fail). Wrapper-lane convergence stays the documented partial fallback.

columns.goal: Close the reproducibility gap where the active ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.codex/config.toml are standalone runtime files that diverge from the envctl-home projected sources with a different model and a different hook set; specify that the projection and lock own or symlink these two runtime files so the rtk hook and future nu-routing cannot drift without moving auth or state out of ~/.claude and ~/.codex.

columns.title: Adopt the profile-owned Nushell MCP tool the nu-syntax skill and the Bash-tool deny gate
columns.goal: Implement the YZXCONV-026 design: package a profile-owned Nushell MCP execution tool (prefer building the profile nu with the mcp feature for nu --mcp else vendor a pure-Rust nu-MCP crate consistent with the no-C trust boundary), vendor a nushell-syntax skill into agent-skills so both Claude and Codex emit idiomatic nu, and add the deny of the built-in Bash tool to both agents through the envctl agent-env projection so nushell is the only sanctioned shell surface. TDD with negative tests that reject a bash escape and prove a nu command runs through the MCP tool.
columns.inputs: YZXCONV-026 design; profile nushell 0.113.1 mcp-feature status; cablehead/mcp-server-nu and ck3mp3r/nu-mcp references; nushell-pro and nushell-skills skill content; envctl agent-env source and lock
columns.target_artifacts: profile-owned Nushell MCP tool; vendored nu-syntax skill in agent-skills; Bash-tool deny agent-env projection; negative and positive routing tests
columns.blocked_paths: adding a C-linked dependency to the trust boundary; global package install; agent auth or state mutation; generated Yazelix runtime hand edits; applying agent-env without preview and review
columns.verification_gate: The profile exposes a Nushell MCP execution tool resolving through the one profile; both agents carry the vendored nu-syntax skill and a Bash-tool deny in their projected config; negative tests prove a bash command is rejected and a nu command runs through the MCP tool; the no-C boundary and single-profile ownership are preserved; a second agent-env preview is idempotent.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore the prior agent-env source lock and projections through the envctl operation receipt re-enable the Bash tool and remove the MCP tool and skill; no auth or state is moved.
columns.next_action: Verified 2026-07-14: profile Nushell 0.113.1 ships the mcp feature (nu --mcp present) so no build or vendored crate is needed; implement on the built-in nu --mcp behind agent-env preview under owner approval.

columns.title: Wire the meta_mcp server into the agent MCP baseline via a profile-owned frontdoor
columns.goal: Make the standalone meta_mcp server (bin meta-mcp, ~30 fleet tools over stdio JSON-RPC, meta_cli-backed) an active agent MCP. Because meta-mcp is a LOCAL stdio binary and agent-env only generates URL-only MCPs that launch no non-profile local binary (the yazelix-mirror rule), wiring it requires FIRST giving it a profile-owned frontdoor: package meta-mcp into the single nix profile (like rtk and yzx), then add it to agent-env.yaml so envctl agent sync projects it identically into .mcp.json (Claude) and .codex/config.toml (Codex), passing the yazelix-mirror gate with a profile-owned command rather than a bunx or other non-profile launcher. TDD.
columns.inputs: meta_mcp crate (bin meta-mcp, ~30 tools); agent-env.yaml yazelix-mirror rule + scripts/tests/test-agent-mcp-yazelix-mirror.sh; yazelix flake packaging; envctl agent-env engine
columns.target_artifacts: profile-packaged meta-mcp frontdoor; agent-env.yaml meta-mcp entry; projected .mcp.json and .codex/config.toml meta-mcp server; mirror-gate proof
columns.blocked_paths: adding a non-profile local-binary MCP that fails the yazelix-mirror gate; global install; agent auth or state mutation; applying agent-env without preview and review
columns.verification_gate: meta-mcp resolves through the single nix profile; agent-env.yaml lists meta-mcp and envctl agent sync projects it identically into .mcp.json and .codex/config.toml; the yazelix-mirror test passes (profile-owned frontdoor, no non-profile launcher); the meta-mcp initialize and tools-list handshake succeeds from the agent; a second sync preview is idempotent.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove the meta-mcp agent-env entry and profile package via the envctl operation receipt; restore the prior lock and projections; meta-mcp stays a standalone unwired binary.
columns.next_action: Package meta-mcp into the single profile, then add it to agent-env.yaml and prove the yazelix-mirror gate plus identical Claude and Codex projection under owner approval.

columns.title: Package the remaining agent supply-chain binaries as flake-declared yazelix runtime packages
columns.goal: Extend the single-profile runtime the yazelix way - each binary is a flake output AND a declared runtime package (as rtk is), not merely present on PATH. Package the remaining agent supply chain into the one profile: claude, codex, grit, icm, weave, bun, bunx, plus the install-gap binaries from YZXCONV-025 (envctl, rusty-idd, teri, hf, nu_plugin, flexnetos_runner) and any remaining supply-chain tools. Verify each resolves through ~/.nix-profile as a flake-declared runtime package (a flake packages.<system>.<name> output consumed by the runtime closure), closing the PATH-ordering drift so the profile front door - not an earlier standalone store bin - owns them. TDD packaging tests.
columns.inputs: YZXCONV-025 profile-binary checklist and install gaps; yazelix flake.nix packaging (rtk and yzx as the pattern); the runtime closure (mkYzx); the PATH-ordering-drift finding
columns.target_artifacts: flake package outputs and runtime-closure entries for each binary; per-binary profile-resolution proof; PATH-ordering-drift closure
columns.blocked_paths: global package-manager install; user-local bin shadows; profile mutation before build proof; generated Yazelix runtime hand edits
columns.verification_gate: Each listed binary (claude, codex, grit, icm, weave, bun, bunx, plus the YZXCONV-025 install gaps) is a flake packages output and a declared runtime-closure package; command -v resolves each through ~/.nix-profile/bin or toolbin (not an earlier standalone store bin); red-then-green packaging tests prove reproducible builds; the one profile remains the sole install owner.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert only the flake packaging additions and tests; reselect the prior profile generation; no user-local shadow is introduced.
columns.next_action: Enumerate the exact binary set from YZXCONV-025, then add flake package outputs and runtime-closure entries the yazelix way (rtk as the template) and prove profile resolution under owner approval.

columns.title: Build the three-lane supply-chain catalog (no-optional, latest-npm ruvector)
columns.goal: Create supply_chain.catalog.csv beside task_graph.source.csv as a SEPARATE registry with a one-way task_id foreign key INTO the task graph (the task graph is never edited and never given supply-chain columns; the reverse task-to-package index is built at generation time). Model three lanes, all yazelix-nix-profile-owned or project-local-pinned and never system or global: path (agent CLIs and tools resolved on PATH via the yazelix runtime closure), service (PostgreSQL plus the RuVector extension, which MUST be flake-declared IN the yazelix nix-profile and run as a profile-owned daemon), and app-dep (libraries the app links, pinned in the app lockfile via a profile-owned toolchain). Keep two independent axes: source_registry (where latest lives) versus install_method (how it is built). Enforce the NO-OPTIONAL LAW: there is no optional, later, or non-blocking state; every row is built before DONE and an agent that defers a row has entered a failure loop; the token optional is illegal as a classification value. Seed ruvector as npm-latest (bun add) in the app-dep lane and as the profile-owned PostgreSQL extension in the service lane; local meta-ruvector and crates.io are reference and lagging fallbacks only. Registry keys come from the secretd broker at build time, never the nix store.
columns.inputs: yazelix_runtime_convergence/SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md; DEC-YZXCONV-007; Architecture Blueprint - LifeOS Core Foundation.md (sha256 014bbebb) lines 87-113; COGNITUM guides (open reference); meta-ruvector plus worktrees (reference); YZXCONV-025 install-gap checklist; YZXCONV-033 packaging pattern
columns.target_artifacts: supply_chain.catalog.csv source of truth; generated/supply_chain.catalog.json; the gen-time task-to-package reverse index; per-lane install-method mapping
columns.blocked_paths: system or global package-manager installs; forcing an app-dep or service item into the path lane or vice versa; nix-profile mutation before build proof; deleting the COGNITUM reference media; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: The catalog validates: every row has a lane in path/service/app-dep, a source_registry, and an install_method; the token optional never appears as a classification value; every task_id foreign key resolves to an existing task; the ruvector rows are source_registry npm with install_method bun-add-latest (app-dep) and a profile-owned PostgreSQL extension (service); no row is in a deferred or optional state; generated/supply_chain.catalog.json regenerates deterministically and the reverse task-to-package index builds.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove supply_chain.catalog.csv and its generated JSON and SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md; the task graph is untouched by construction (one-way foreign key), so no task-CSV rollback is needed.
columns.next_action: Scaffold supply_chain.catalog.csv with the three-lane schema and the rtk and ruvector seed rows, wire the gen-time reverse join, then enumerate further from the open COGNITUM and meta-ruvector references as needed.

columns.title: Package PostgreSQL and the RuVector extension INTO the yazelix nix-profile (service lane)
columns.owner_agent: Yazelix Packaging Agent / Database Runtime Agent
columns.goal: Close the owner-identified current-state gap that no database is in the yazelix nix-profile: the databases MUST be in the yazelix nix-profile to pull this off. Declare PostgreSQL and the RuVector PostgreSQL extension as flake outputs in the yazelix runtime closure (the same the-yazelix-way mechanism as rtk), so the single profile owns the whole stack (blueprint Phase 4 build the entire stack, musl-static, drop the nix store). RuVector loads as a profile-provided extension into the profile-provided postgres via shared_preload_libraries with a data dir kept outside the store. Latest RuVector is the npm-published build (200-plus agent tools); crates.io lags and local meta-ruvector is the napi-exhaust scratchpad reference only. No system postgres, no apt, no global install. TDD packaging tests.
columns.inputs: Architecture Blueprint lines 68-79 (RuVector as a PostgreSQL extension) and 23-42 (Phase 4 Nix plus musl); yazelix flake.nix packaging (rtk and yzx as the pattern) and the runtime closure (mkYzx); SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md service lane; DEC-YZXCONV-007; the latest RuVector npm and extension build
columns.target_artifacts: flake packages outputs for postgresql and the ruvector extension; yazelix runtime-closure entries; a profile-owned postgres-plus-ruvector service proof; a data-dir-outside-the-store layout
columns.blocked_paths: system or apt postgres; global installs; profile mutation before build proof; committing a database data dir or secrets into the repo or the nix store; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: postgresql and the ruvector extension are flake packages outputs and declared yazelix runtime-closure packages; both resolve through the nix profile (not a system or apt postgres); a profile-owned postgres starts with the ruvector extension loaded (present in pg_available_extensions and CREATE EXTENSION ruvector succeeds) against a data dir outside the store; red-then-green packaging tests prove a reproducible build; the one profile is the sole install owner.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert only the flake packaging additions and tests and reselect the prior profile generation; leave any existing external database untouched; no system postgres is introduced.
columns.next_action: After the catalog service-lane rows exist, add flake package outputs for postgresql and the ruvector extension and a runtime-closure entry the yazelix way, then prove CREATE EXTENSION ruvector from a profile-owned postgres under owner approval.

columns.title: Seed and freeze the full store inventory as the catalog-before-run target gate (per-artifact verified leading registry)
columns.goal: Populate AND FREEZE supply_chain.catalog.csv with the complete verified store inventory (SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md section 8) as the CATALOG-BEFORE-RUN target gate: per owner ratification DEC-YZXCONV-009 the supply-chain target for every artifact is set in the catalog BEFORE any downstream supply-chain task (035, 037, 038) executes, because the source with the latest code wins and the code is the source of truth. Add every required row across three lanes. service: SC-postgres (the consolidation target and permanent source of truth), SC-ruvector-postgres (the pgrx extension carrying the 200-plus SQL agent tools, loaded into postgres), SC-libsql-remote (secrets store, remote-only no-C), and SC-ruvllm pending its lane ruling. app-dep: SC-redb with the DUAL-MODE note (pass-through in the happy path and a durable retaining WAL when postgres is down, plus dedup and geometry-engine roles), SC-ruvector-core, SC-ruvector-sona, SC-ruvector-npm as the ONLY npm-latest-governed binding, SC-rvf for AgentDB and the rvf format, SC-hnsw-rs, SC-sqlx-sqlite, SC-candle-or-ort, and SC-fastembed. path: SC-nu-plugin-ruvector, SC-beads, SC-weave (dual sqlite and libsql), and SC-embed-minilm as a model asset. For EVERY row record the VERIFIED LEADING REGISTRY resolved live at pin time (crates.io leads the ruvector engine; npm carries only the front-end binding; the at-ruvnet-slash-ruvector handle is dead) into source_registry plus source_locator plus pinned_version, keep classification required for every row, and add audit rows for kache (compiler cache, not a data store) and the codex-harness rusqlite-bundled C-SQLite caveat so the no-C boundary stays auditable. Downstream tasks CONSUME this frozen target and never re-derive it; a task that installs, packages, or links an artifact whose catalog target is unset or unverified is a NO-OPTIONAL black-hole violation and fails closed.
columns.inputs: SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md section 8 inventory; DEC-YZXCONV-007 and DEC-YZXCONV-008 and DEC-YZXCONV-009 (owner catalog-before-run ratification); the 2026-07-14 deep-research reports (codebase store scan, blueprint cross-ref, live registry verification); Architecture Blueprint lines 44-48 and 245-252 and 361-439
columns.target_artifacts: populated and frozen supply_chain.catalog.csv covering all lanes with a per-artifact pinned_version; a catalog freeze marker (pinned_at plus per-row verified registry); generated/supply_chain.catalog.json; the task-to-package reverse index
columns.blocked_paths: promoting the codex-harness bundled C-SQLite into the profile; forcing an item into the wrong lane; applying the npm-latest rule to independent crates such as redb candle sqlx or libsql; system or global installs; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: supply_chain.catalog.csv contains every SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN section 8 row with a valid lane and source_registry and install_method; EVERY row carries a source_locator and a pinned_version verified live at pin time (per-artifact leading registry, never a blanket npm rule); the catalog is frozen before any downstream supply-chain task runs so 035 and 037 and 038 consume the pinned target and never re-derive it; redb carries the dual-mode WAL note; ruvector-postgres is the service-lane 200-plus-SQL-tool extension; SC-ruvector-npm is the only row on the npm-latest rule and uses a live handle and never at-ruvnet-slash-ruvector; no row is optional or deferred; the kache and rusqlite-bundled audit rows are present and flagged; generated/supply_chain.catalog.json regenerates deterministically and the reverse index builds.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove the added catalog rows and regenerate; the task graph is untouched by construction (one-way foreign key).
columns.next_action: Write the section-8 rows into supply_chain.catalog.csv with per-artifact verified registries and pinned versions and the dual-mode redb note, freeze the target, then regenerate the catalog JSON and reverse index so 035 037 and 038 consume the frozen pins.

columns.title: Hard-gate the single nix-profile with failing flake plus live-box checks (close the un-enforced single-owner rule)
columns.owner_agent: Yazelix Packaging Agent / Runtime Verification Agent
columns.goal: The single-nix-profile rule (PLAN.md clause 1 and 14) is CONVERGED (YZXCONV-003/006/014/022) and audited (YZXCONV-020) but NOT hard-gated by a failing check the way Kache (YZXCONV-016) and Nushell (YZXCONV-017) are, so a live violation is never caught. Deep yazelix review 2026-07-14 proved two gaps: (1) the one derivation-level foundation gate flexnetos_foundation_contracts (flake.nix, asserts exactly one .desktop, no shadow launcher, all runtime paths under ~/.nix-profile) is NEVER built by CI (ci.yml and version_gate.yml omit it; nix flake check --no-build only evaluates, never realizes it), and (2) EVERY check validates the derivation, never the LIVE BOX - nothing runs nix profile list, nothing scans PATH or ~/.local or ~/.cargo or /usr or npm-global for shadow installs, nothing verifies the resolved binary comes from ~/.nix-profile. Close both, red-first per the 016/017 idiom: (A) wire .#checks.x86_64-linux.flexnetos_foundation_contracts and contracts into the ci.yml build list so the one-desktop/no-shadow/profile-path assertions FAIL a PR; (B) add a new derivation flake check single_profile_closure (closureInfo over lifeos_foundation_yzx) asserting the foundation owns the union of yzx plus every flexnetosExecutables key with exactly one yzx and nothing resolving to a non-foundation store path; (C) add a live-box gate nushell/system/profile_guard.nu wired like host_policy.nu into the yazelix_host_policy service that FAILS when nix profile list is not exactly the one foundation profile, when any fixed-set binary resolves outside ~/.nix-profile, when ~/.nix-profile/bin is not PATH-first, or when a second terminal .desktop exists; (D) reuse the repo fakeYazelix and fakeYazelixDesktop negative fixtures to prove the gate itself FAILS on a two-desktop or shadow-binary tree. No profile mutation to force green.
columns.inputs: deep yazelix single-profile review 2026-07-14 (flake.nix:932-960 four installable roots, 1236-1364 flexnetos_foundation_contracts, 1203-1231 runtime_contracts, 977-995 fakeYazelix fixtures; ci.yml:45-53; version_gate.yml:50-54; runtime/yzx/doctor.rs; nushell/system/host_policy.nu); PLAN.md clause 1 and 14; the YZXCONV-016 and YZXCONV-017 red-gate idiom; YZXCONV-003/014/022 convergence receipts

Add a thin redb table-buffer verb only if the existing capture path does not already stream parsed code blocks. TDD.

columns.title: Database-Hosted Codebase / Semantic IDE core - code lives in Postgres as AST fragments plus embeddings plus causal edges, edited via SQL (ARCHITECT-SURFACED GAP)

Host the codebase IN Postgres plus RuVector as a knowledge graph (blueprint 316-329, 445-447, 522-528, 581-602, 685-690): each row carries raw_code, module_path, AST fragments, semantic_embedding, and causal dependency edges. Agents locate functions by MEANING via RuVector hybrid search (SELECT raw_code, module_path FROM codebase_table WHERE language=rust AND status=production ORDER BY semantic_embedding by distance to a target vector LIMIT 5, blueprint 589-594) and rewrite directly with UPDATE statements on the rows (152, 594), solving the context-window limit (the Semantic IDE, 685-686). Concurrency is in-DB Copy-On-Write branching (344-346, 494-498, 596-598): clone rows into isolated staging branches, agents edit sandboxed, only verified branches merge to the main trunk, failures roll back (no dirty repo). Define the schema and the semantic-edit plus COW capabilities; leverage nu_plugin_codedb schema/materialize and ruvector-postgres (230-plus SQL fns). TDD.

columns.title: Swarm prediction and routing - ATAS/ESN forecasting plus RuvLTRA routing plus Ruflo coordination of AgentDB (.rvf) agents
columns.goal: Account for the blueprint swarm-orchestration layer (127-137, 163-215, 270-290, 292-314, 441-509, 551-559, 640-661). ATAS (Agentic Temporal Attractor Studio) forecasts project trajectories with Echo-State Networks / Reservoir Computing (a fixed random reservoir plus a trained linear readout, cheap on ruvllm), running ensemble simulations up to 100 steps ahead to produce Future Timelines (State Diffs, Resource Burn, Complexity Spikes, Failure Nodes) and detect a trajectory spiraling out of bounds before any production commit. RuvLTRA routes tasks by Proportional Intelligence via a trained classifier head (659): reflex tasks to local ruvllm, architectural/security to cloud Opus/GPT-4, steering the swarm toward the most stable timeline. AGENTDB (owner emphasis): Ruflo is the meta-harness swarm coordinator, and its agents are NOT massive individual models - each Ruflo agent is deployed as a single self-contained AgentDB .rvf cognitive container running on top of the ONE frozen foundation model hosted by ruvllm on the edge (blueprint 127-137, 551-559). Each .rvf gives the agent unique intelligence via three components: MicroLoRA adapters (small rank-1/2 parameter weights that hot-swap into the base model in milliseconds for skill and personality), SONA (a Self-Optimizing Neural Architecture learning loop that tracks the agent successes and failures and adjusts behavior to avoid repeating mistakes, via active RL - Thompson Sampling, Q-Learning, PPO, line 272), and FastGRNN routing (a lightweight graph of the agent memory that pre-filters exactly what the LLM must focus on). Ruflo spins up parallel swarms of these .rvf agents to debate, build, and test alternate timelines, and RuvLTRA routes each .rvf agent by complexity; the .rvf deploy and hot-swap machinery itself is YZXCONV-044, which this task consumes. Leverage the teri ComputeWorld predict-before-execute twin (FlexNetOS/teri, the current ATAS analog, section 8e/11) and meta-ruvector. TDD.
columns.inputs: Architecture Blueprint 127-137, 163-215, 270-290, 292-314, 441-509, 551-559, 640-661; AgentDB/.rvf cognitive containers (MicroLoRA rank-1/2 + SONA + FastGRNN; active RL Thompson/Q-Learning/PPO line 272); YZXCONV-044 .rvf deploy/hot-swap machinery (SC-rvf/SC-ruvllm); teri ComputeWorld (git FlexNetOS/teri, section 11 integration evidence); ruvllm (ESN readout on the edge, one frozen model); RuvLTRA trained classifier head; Ruflo (revnet/ruflo)
columns.target_artifacts: an ATAS/ESN ensemble forecaster (Future Timelines with the four data points); a RuvLTRA proportional router (trained classifier head); a Ruflo swarm coordinator; a spiral-out-of-bounds early-failure detection proof
columns.blocked_paths: committing production code before the winning timeline is selected; routing every task to a cloud model (defeats proportional intelligence); global installs; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: Red tests first FAIL when there is no ensemble forecast (no Future Timelines with State Diffs/Resource Burn/Complexity Spikes/Failure Nodes), when RuvLTRA sends a reflex task to a cloud model or an architectural task to a local reflex model, or when a spiraling trajectory is not caught before commit; when a Ruflo agent is deployed as a monolithic model rather than a self-contained AgentDB .rvf container hot-swapping MicroLoRA/SONA/FastGRNN onto the one frozen ruvllm model, when the SONA loop does not adjust ranking after a failed step; then ATAS/ESN forecasts N-step timelines over swarms of .rvf AgentDB agents (each on the shared frozen model), SONA feeds success and failure back into ranking, RuvLTRA routes by complexity via its classifier head, Ruflo coordinates the ensemble, and an out-of-bounds trajectory is flagged before any production commit.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove the ATAS/RuvLTRA/Ruflo wiring added under test; no timeline is committed.
columns.next_action: Leverage teri ComputeWorld as the ATAS core, add the RuvLTRA classifier router and the Ruflo coordinator, and prove early failure detection under owner approval.

columns.title: Defense/Integrity layer - MinCut immune system plus SHAKE256 Witness Chains plus Causal Graph Verify (ARCHITECT-SURFACED GAP)
columns.goal: Architect-surfaced obvious gap: the blueprint Phase-3 swarm immune system, distinct from the prediction/routing of YZXCONV-045. Three mathematical guardrails (20-21, 206-215, 331-346, 500-509, 604-617): (1) RuVector MinCut - treat agent communications and code dependencies as a graph and, in subpolynomial O(n^o(1)) time (arXiv:2512.13105), sever a hallucinating or drifting agent in milliseconds before contagion; leverage meta-ruvector ruvector-mincut (present, section 8e/11). (2) Cryptographic Witness Chains (SHAKE256) - bind every code edit or fact in a .rvf to a SHAKE256 audit trail rooted in a source vector so the AI cannot bluff a dependency; the rvagent-engine ledger exists but uses BLAKE3 today, and SHAKE256 is the blueprint target (section 10 gap). (3) Causal Graph Verification - a GNN scans for a causal edge for every claim and the graph rejects any hallucinated endpoint lacking a historical root. TDD.
columns.inputs: Architecture Blueprint 20-21, 63-66, 202-215, 331-346, 500-509, 604-617; meta-ruvector ruvector-mincut (arXiv:2512.13105) plus rvagent-engine witness ledger (section 8e/11, currently BLAKE3); AgentDB/.rvf; the codebase graph (YZXCONV-043)
columns.target_artifacts: a MinCut runtime immune gate that severs a drifting agent; a SHAKE256 witness-chain binding on .rvf edits/facts; a GNN causal-edge verifier that rejects rootless claims
columns.blocked_paths: accepting a claim with no causal root; leaving a hallucinating agent connected; using BLAKE3 where the blueprint mandates SHAKE256 for the witness trail; global installs; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: Red tests first FAIL when a hallucinating agent is not severed by MinCut, when a code edit is accepted without a SHAKE256 witness link to a source vector, or when a claim with no causal edge is admitted; then MinCut severs a drifting agent deterministically within the target time, every .rvf edit carries a SHAKE256 witness chain to its source vector, and Causal Graph Verification rejects a rootless (hallucinated) endpoint.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove the MinCut/witness/causal wiring added under test; no swarm state altered.
columns.next_action: Wire ruvector-mincut as the runtime immune gate, upgrade the rvagent witness ledger to SHAKE256, and add GNN causal-edge verification under owner approval.

columns.title: Build the LifeOS TypeScript/Bun UI (bun add ruvector, FFI, reads shared redb, no HTTP) - the app YZXCONV-041 launches
columns.goal: Account for the blueprint Run/front-end (27, 91, 111, 356-358, 673, 691-692): build the LifeOS user interface as a TypeScript UI run on Bun. Use bun add ruvector so Bun runs the compiled native Rust extension directly in the event loop with zero network latency via FFI (111, 356). LifeOS reads the SAME memory-mapped redb state files the agents write to render swarm status, and communicates with the Yazelix/Zellij workspace over UDS or shared-redb, NEVER HTTP/REST (122-125, 692). This is the APP that YZXCONV-041 launches as the primary UI (041 is the Mars-to-LifeOS launch/transport binding; 049 builds the app itself). TDD.
columns.inputs: Architecture Blueprint 27, 91, 111, 122-125, 356-358, 673, 691-692; YZXCONV-041 Mars-to-LifeOS binding; SC-ruvector-npm (bun add ruvector, YZXCONV-036); SC-redb shared buffer; bun (path lane)
columns.target_artifacts: a LifeOS TypeScript/Bun UI; a bun add ruvector FFI integration; a shared-mmap-redb read path for swarm status; a no-HTTP UDS/shared-redb client
columns.blocked_paths: an HTTP/REST client on the LifeOS-to-Yazelix seam; a private redb copy instead of the shared buffer; a Node global install or npm -g (project-local bun only); agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: Red tests first FAIL when the LifeOS UI uses HTTP/REST to reach the workspace, when it reads a private redb copy instead of the shared mmap file the agents write, or when ruvector is loaded over the network rather than bun add ruvector FFI; then the LifeOS TypeScript UI runs on Bun, executes the native ruvector extension in-process via FFI with zero network, renders swarm status from the shared mmap redb, and speaks only UDS or shared-redb to Yazelix.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove the LifeOS UI build added under test; no runtime state moved.
columns.next_action: After the Mars-to-LifeOS binding (YZXCONV-041), build the Bun/TS UI with bun add ruvector FFI and the shared-redb swarm-status read under owner approval.

columns.title: no-HTTP/REST invariant gate - the LifeOS-to-Yazelix seam and agent loops use UDS or shared-redb only
columns.goal: Make the blueprint no-HTTP/REST rule a FAILING gate, not just prose. Blueprint 122-125 and 692: LifeOS avoids standard HTTP/REST to talk to the Yazelix/Zellij terminal workspace and instead connects via Unix Domain Sockets OR by reading the shared memory-mapped redb state files; the agents likewise stream into memory, not over the network. Red-first (the YZXCONV-016/017 idiom): a check FAILS whenever any HTTP or REST or TCP listener/client appears on the LifeOS-to-Yazelix seam (YZXCONV-041/049) or on an agent hot loop, and passes only when the sole transports are UDS or shared mmap redb. This closes the audit finding that no-HTTP was an ungated invariant. TDD.
columns.inputs: Architecture Blueprint 122-125, 356-358, 692; YZXCONV-041 Mars-to-LifeOS binding; YZXCONV-049 LifeOS UI; the coverage-audit invariant-gating finding 2026-07-14 (no-HTTP ungated)
columns.target_artifacts: a failing no-HTTP/REST gate over the LifeOS-to-Yazelix seam and agent loops; a UDS-or-shared-redb-only transport assertion; a negative fixture (an HTTP listener) that makes the gate FAIL
columns.blocked_paths: adding an HTTP or REST or TCP listener/client on the LifeOS-to-Yazelix seam or an agent hot loop; weakening the gate to allow a loopback HTTP shim; global installs; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: Red tests first FAIL when any HTTP/REST/TCP listener or client exists on the LifeOS-to-Yazelix seam or an agent hot loop, and when an HTTP-listener negative fixture is NOT rejected; then the only transports on that seam are UDS or shared mmap redb, the negative fixture makes the gate FAIL, and the gate is idempotent.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert the no-HTTP gate and fixtures added under test; no runtime seam changed.
columns.next_action: Write the HTTP-listener negative fixture, then assert UDS-or-shared-redb-only on the LifeOS-to-Yazelix seam under owner approval.

columns.title: no-sidecar invariant gate - RuVector runs as a native Postgres extension, no external vector-DB sidecar
columns.goal: Make the blueprint No-Sidecar rule a FAILING gate. Blueprint 68, 76-79, 241, 424: RuVector executes a Trojan-Horse strategy by embedding vector search and GNN capabilities directly into PostgreSQL as native Rust extensions, eliminating the latency and sync overhead of a separate relational-plus-vector sidecar; all AI operations run where the data lives via 230-plus SQL functions. Red-first: a check FAILS whenever a separate or external vector database or sidecar process is present, or when vector search runs outside Postgres; passes only when RuVector is loaded as a Postgres extension (shared_preload_libraries, CREATE EXTENSION ruvector) and every vector/GNN operation executes in-DB with no sidecar. This closes the audit finding that no-sidecar was ungated, and reinforces the service lane (YZXCONV-035). TDD.
columns.inputs: Architecture Blueprint 68, 76-79, 241-243, 424; YZXCONV-035 postgres-plus-ruvector-in-profile; the coverage-audit invariant-gating finding 2026-07-14 (no-sidecar ungated)
columns.target_artifacts: a failing no-sidecar gate; an in-Postgres-only vector-search assertion; a negative fixture (an external vector-DB process) that makes the gate FAIL
columns.blocked_paths: running a separate/external vector database or sidecar process; performing vector search outside Postgres; global installs; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: Red tests first FAIL when a separate or external vector-DB or sidecar process exists, when vector search runs outside Postgres, or when an external-vector-DB negative fixture is NOT rejected; then RuVector is loaded as a Postgres extension, all vector/GNN operations execute in-DB, the sidecar negative fixture makes the gate FAIL, and the gate is idempotent.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert the no-sidecar gate and fixtures added under test; no service changed.
columns.next_action: After the postgres-plus-ruvector service lane (YZXCONV-035), write the external-vector-DB negative fixture and assert in-Postgres-only vector search under owner approval.

columns.title: Bump kache to the verified latest and apply the latest-wins law to the WHOLE path lane (fix the 0.8.0 staleness miss)
columns.owner_agent: Supply-Chain Catalog Agent / Yazelix Packaging Agent
columns.goal: The yazelix profile pins kache 0.8.0 (packaging/kache_release.nix:3, source github:kunobi-ninja/kache) - STALE. Root cause: the verified-leader latest-wins law (DEC-YZXCONV-009) was wrongly scoped to the ruvector supply chain, and kache was exempted as a SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN section 8d audit-note (a compiler cache, not a data store), so its staleness was never verified against latest. Fix, TDD: (1) SOURCE IDENTITY FIRST - the reachable verified latest on the pinned source is v0.10.0 (github:kunobi-ninja/kache tags top at v0.10.0; crates.io kache tops at 0.10.0; npm kache 0.1.0 is an unrelated package); OWNER-CONFIRMED 2026-07-14: the canonical source is github:kunobi-ninja/kache and the pin target is v0.10.0; the earlier v2.1.1 was a NAME COLLISION - the owner had pulled the WRONG project github.com/MayakaApps/Kache (a Kotlin caching library), not this Rust RUSTC_WRAPPER kache - so v2.1.1 is REJECTED and the source identity is settled. (2) Bump kache_release.nix to the confirmed verified-latest and recompute srcHash plus cargoHash. (3) BROADEN the law: latest-wins applies to EVERY profile-owned artifact (path, service, app-dep), not just the ruvector engine - delete the section-8d audit-note exemption; every path-lane tool (kache, rtk, beads_rust, weave, nu, carapace, ...) carries a per-artifact verified-latest pin resolved at pin time.
columns.inputs: packaging/kache_release.nix (version 0.8.0, owner kunobi-ninja, repo kache, srcHash/cargoHash); reports/verification-2026-07-14 (kache --version=0.8.0 installed); DEC-YZXCONV-009 verified-leader/latest-wins law; SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN section 8c path lane + 8d audit notes; owner-confirmed 2026-07-14: canonical source github:kunobi-ninja/kache, pin v0.10.0; v2.1.1 was the wrong project github.com/MayakaApps/Kache (Kotlin lib, name collision, rejected)
columns.target_artifacts: kache_release.nix bumped to the confirmed verified-latest with rebuilt srcHash+cargoHash; the section-8d kache exemption removed and kache promoted to an SC-kache path-lane row; a per-artifact verified-latest pin on every path-lane tool; a resolved kache source-identity note
columns.blocked_paths: pinning a kache version from the wrong project (e.g. the github.com/MayakaApps/Kache v2.1.1 Kotlin-lib name collision) or a version no source proves; leaving kache at 0.8.0; keeping any profile-owned path tool exempt from latest-verification; global installs; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: Red tests first FAIL when kache resolves to 0.8.0 or any non-latest version, when the kache source identity is unverified, when a pinned version is not provable on a real source, or when any profile-owned path-lane tool lacks a verified-latest pin; then kache_release.nix pins v0.10.0 from the owner-confirmed source github:kunobi-ninja/kache (never github.com/MayakaApps/Kache) with rebuilt srcHash+cargoHash, the section-8d exemption is gone, and every path-lane tool carries a per-artifact verified-latest pin.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert kache_release.nix to the checksum-backed prior pin and restore the prior profile generation; keep the catalog note of the intended latest.
columns.next_action: Bump kache_release.nix 0.8.0 to v0.10.0 (github:kunobi-ninja/kache, owner-confirmed) + rebuild srcHash and cargoHash, and add verified-latest pins across the path lane under owner approval.

columns.title: Audit and update the ENTIRE supply chain to the verified leader - every catalog row, all lanes, no exemptions
columns.goal: Owner mandate 2026-07-14: the ENTIRE supply chain must go through the verified leader - e.g. wild (the linker), bun, bunx, redb, PostgreSQL, SQLite, and the rest (rtk, nu, carapace, beads_rust, weave, ruvector-core/sona/postgres, ruvllm, candle, libsql, kache) - everything audited and updated to latest. Generalize the DEC-YZXCONV-009 verified-leader / latest-wins law from the ruvector engine to EVERY profile-owned or project-local-pinned artifact across all three lanes (path, service, app-dep). For every supply_chain.catalog.csv row: resolve the canonical source, verify the leading version live at audit time (per-artifact leading registry, no bluffed versions - the kache v0.8.0-while-v0.10.0-was-latest miss and the ruvector at-ruvnet-slash-ruvector dead-handle are the precedents), and UPDATE the pin at its real site (flake input, Cargo.toml, bun lockfile, kache_release.nix, pgrx pin, etc.) to that verified latest. Remove EVERY audit-note exemption - no artifact is grandfathered on an old version; a stale pin is a NO-OPTIONAL violation. The audit is re-runnable on cadence so the chain never drifts. TDD plus an audit-report artifact.
columns.inputs: owner universal-verified-leader mandate 2026-07-14 (wild, bun, bunx, redb, PostgreSQL, SQLite, and the rest - all audited and updated); DEC-YZXCONV-009 (previously ruvector-scoped) and DEC-YZXCONV-013 (nu_plugin C-leak precedent); the kache 0.8.0-to-latest miss (YZXCONV-052); SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN section 8 (all rows) and section 7.1 canonical source handles (npm ~ruvnet, crates.io/users/ruvnet, github ruvnet + cognitum-one, cognitum.one); yazelix flake.nix inputs + packaging pins; envctl Cargo.toml pins
columns.target_artifacts: a supply-chain audit report (every row: canonical source, current pin, verified latest, action, proof); updated pins across flake inputs, Cargo.toml, lockfiles, and release.nix; a re-runnable audit; zero remaining audit-note exemptions
columns.blocked_paths: grandfathering any artifact on a non-latest pin; keeping an audit-note exemption; pinning a version no reachable source proves; a blanket registry assumption instead of a per-artifact verified leader; global installs; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: Red tests first FAIL when any catalog row lacks a live-verified latest pin, when any artifact (wild, bun, bunx, redb, postgres, sqlite, rtk, nu, carapace, beads, weave, ruvector-core/sona/postgres, ruvllm, candle, libsql, kache, or the rest) is grandfathered on an old version, when an audit-note exemption remains, or when a pin cites a version no source proves; then every path/service/app-dep row resolves its canonical source and pins the verified leading version, all pins are updated at their real sites, no exemption remains, and the audit re-runs deterministically.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert to the checksum-backed prior pins for any row whose bump fails to build and reselect the prior profile generation; keep the audit report of the intended verified latest.
columns.next_action: Enumerate every catalog row, resolve each canonical source, verify the live leader, update every pin at its real site, and re-run the audit on cadence under owner approval.

columns.title: Yazelix-provision the ICM config so the code model and durable caches survive a full reprovision
columns.goal: The live fix set ~/.config/icm/config.toml to model = jinaai/jina-embeddings-v2-base-code (768d, code-optimized) and yazelix PR 73 moved HF_HOME, TORCH_HOME, PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH, STARSHIP_CACHE and the icm model cache onto durable ~/.cache. Those persist across a reboot but are per-machine home state that a FULL reprovision would wipe - icm would revert to the config/default.toml intfloat/multilingual-e5-base default. Provision the icm config reproducibly from yazelix: render ~/.config/icm/config.toml (or bake the code-optimized model into the fleet icm config/default.toml) plus the durable-cache env, so a freshly reprovisioned box comes up on the code model with a durable, warm embedding cache. No global installs; stay inside the profile.
columns.inputs: live fix ~/.config/icm/config.toml = jinaai/jina-embeddings-v2-base-code; yazelix PR 73 (durable HF/torch/playwright/starship + icm cache in nushell/system/volatile_runtime.nu); icm config resolution order ICM_CONFIG then ~/.config/icm/config.toml then baked config/default.toml; fastembed downloads via HF_HOME which is the real model cache
columns.target_artifacts: yazelix provisioning that renders the icm config (code-optimized model) and durable-cache env reproducibly; a fresh reprovision yields the code model plus a durable warm cache with no manual step
columns.blocked_paths: global or user-home installs outside the profile; committing secrets; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: Red tests first FAIL when a simulated fresh reprovision does not produce ~/.config/icm/config.toml pinning the code-optimized model and the durable-cache env; then yazelix provisioning renders both deterministically and the reprovision comes up on the code model with a durable cache.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert the yazelix provisioning change; the per-machine ~/.config/icm/config.toml from the live fix remains as the interim durable state.
columns.next_action: Provision ~/.config/icm/config.toml (or the fleet icm default) plus the durable-cache env from yazelix and prove it survives a simulated full reprovision.

columns.title: Add the ICM PostgreSQL backend option as a durable remote memory store
columns.goal: icm defaults to a local SQLite memories.db but supports a remote backend via ICM_DB_BACKEND=postgres (per icm embed --db and the store config; opensearch is the other remote option). Evaluate and enable icm on PostgreSQL so memories live in the shared project Postgres instead of a per-home SQLite file, aligning with the blueprint direction of RuVector-in-Postgres and the no-sidecar/no-HTTP runtime. The postgres path MUST honor the envctl no-C trust boundary: pure-Rust ring-only client, libSQL-remote or a C-free postgres driver, no aws-lc, no bundled C SQLite. Keep the SQLite default as fallback; make postgres an opt-in, migration-safe option.
columns.inputs: icm embed --help (ICM_DB_BACKEND selects postgres or opensearch remote backend; --db overrides SQLite); icm store config in config/default.toml; blueprint Postgres-hosted codebase plus RuVector-as-native-extension direction; envctl no-C boundary (ring-only rustls, libSQL remote, pure-Rust crypto)
columns.target_artifacts: an evaluated, documented ICM PostgreSQL backend option (ICM_DB_BACKEND=postgres) with a C-free driver path; a migration from the local SQLite memories.db; SQLite retained as default fallback
columns.blocked_paths: linking C SQLite or aws-lc into the icm postgres path; global installs; deleting the existing SQLite memories.db before a verified migration; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: Red tests first FAIL when the ICM PostgreSQL backend is not reachable via ICM_DB_BACKEND=postgres with a C-free client, when the SQLite default is not preserved as fallback, or when the postgres path pulls a banned C dependency; then icm runs on PostgreSQL with a pure-Rust ring-only client, memories migrate from SQLite, and the no-C boundary holds.
columns.rollback_plan: Fall back to the default local SQLite memories.db with ICM_DB_BACKEND unset; keep the postgres evaluation report.
columns.next_action: Evaluate ICM_DB_BACKEND=postgres with a C-free client, prove the no-C boundary, migrate memories from SQLite, and keep SQLite as the fallback default.
